description: Phase 3 — Advanced git + ship → RELEASE LINE (v1.0 = Core Parity) (PLAN.md §9; ADR-0003 mutations/auth via shell-out git tier; ADR-0006 reuse system git signing/credentials; ADR-0007 licensing gate). Adds the advanced git surface on top of Phase 2: 3-pane merge conflict resolver, interactive rebase, GPG+SSH commit signing, worktrees, reflog, bisect, custom commands, external diff/merge tools; plus the two release-blockers — GitHub PR creation (the one forge at v1) and the licensing gate (trial + offline signed-key verify). EXIT: Core Parity reached — Lady is feature-complete vs Fork's daily-use surface and ready for the first public release. Fast-follow (NOT here): LFS, git-flow, submodule edge cases, PR creation for GitLab/Bitbucket/Azure DevOps. Actual packaging/notarization/auto-update is Phase 6. No AI (Phase 5). Branches from main (Phase 2 must be complete + merged to main first). Each story = one context-window commit; after any mutation the UI refreshes status + refs + graph (PLAN.md §3.2). Mutating/engine methods MUST be unit-tested against throwaway tempdir repos (system git permitted for fixtures, ADR-0003). UI stories rely on build + typecheck + unit tests for loop verification; a manual window check is noted in progress.txt.

userStories.title: Conflict model + resolve engine (lady-diff::merge)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to detect and resolve merge/rebase conflicts programmatically so the UI can drive resolution.
acceptanceCriteria: lady-diff::merge: parse a conflicted file into ConflictRegions { base, ours, theirs } (diff3-style markers) plus the surrounding context; Conflicted files come from Phase 2 status() ChangeKind::Conflicted

lady-git: resolve ops — take_ours(path) / take_theirs(path) / write_resolution(path, bytes), then mark_resolved(path) (git add) and conflict_state(repo) (in-merge / in-rebase / in-cherry-pick) + abort()
Tauri commands for list-conflicts / take-ours / take-theirs / write-resolution / mark-resolved / abort

Unit test: create a real merge conflict in a tempdir repo, parse regions, take-ours, mark resolved, assert the file is unconflicted and staged; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green; cargo deny passes
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §4 lady-diff::merge, §6.5. Foundation for the 3-pane UI and interactive-rebase conflict handoff.

userStories.title: 3-pane merge conflict resolver UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to resolve conflicts visually with base/ours/theirs/result.
acceptanceCriteria: ui: a resolver view with base | ours | theirs panes and an editable result pane; per-hunk 'take ours' / 'take theirs' / edit; conflict minimap markers on the scrollbar (PLAN.md §1)
Resolving writes the result then marks the file resolved (PH3-001 commands); progresses to the next conflicted file; completing all enables continue/commit
Surfaces automatically when a Phase 2 merge / cherry-pick / revert (or PH3-004 rebase) reports conflicts
npm run build green; cargo build green; manual window verification noted in progress.txt
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §6.5. Reuses PH3-001 engine + Phase 1 DiffView styling. Never auto-write a resolution without showing it.

userStories.title: Interactive rebase plan model + executor (engine)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reliable interactive-rebase engine driven from a structured plan.
acceptanceCriteria: lady-git: RebasePlan = ordered Vec<RebaseStep { oid, action: Pick|Reword|Edit|Squash|Fixup|Drop, message: Option<String> }>; reorder is encoded by the vec order

rebase_interactive(repo, onto, plan) executes by driving `git rebase -i` with a generated todo + a GIT_SEQUENCE_EDITOR shim (and GIT_EDITOR for reword); supports stop-on-conflict with continue() / skip() / abort() and reports current rebase state
Robust recovery: a conflict leaves the repo mid-rebase and conflict_state() reports it (hands off to PH3-001/002); abort() restores the original HEAD
Unit tests against tempdir repos: squash two commits; drop one; reorder two; a conflicting reorder stops then aborts cleanly; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green; cargo deny passes
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §6.4 — drive real git rebase for correctness. The sequence-editor shim can be a tiny helper binary or a `sh -c` writing the todo. Test continue/abort paths hard.

userStories.title: Interactive rebase UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to visually edit, reorder, and squash commits.
acceptanceCriteria: ui: an interactive-rebase editor listing the selected commit range with a per-commit action picker (pick/reword/edit/squash/fixup/drop) and drag-to-reorder; reword opens a message editor
Run executes PH3-003; on conflict it hands off to the 3-pane resolver (PH3-002) with continue / skip / abort controls; on success the graph + refs refresh
Entry points: commit context-menu 'Rebase interactive from here' and a branch action
npm run build green; cargo build green; manual window verification noted in progress.txt
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §1 interactive rebase. Ties PH3-003 to PH3-002.

userStories.title: Commit signing (GPG + SSH)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to sign my commits and see signature status.
acceptanceCriteria: lady-git: commit signing honors the user's git config (commit.gpgsign, gpg.format, gpg.program / gpg.ssh.* per ADR-0006); a per-commit 'sign' toggle can force -S; passphrase prompts surface via the credential UI
Signature verification status surfaced on commits (parse `git log --show-signature` / %G? -> Good|Bad|None|Untrusted) and shown as a badge in commit details + graph row

SSH-signing unit test: generate an ephemeral ssh key + allowed-signers in a tempdir, set gpg.format=ssh, sign a commit, assert verification = Good; GPG path wired + manually verifiable (note in progress.txt)
cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §1 + §6.6, ADR-0006. SSH signing is fully testable in the loop; GPG needs a keyring so note manual verification.

userStories.title: Worktrees
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create and manage worktrees.
acceptanceCriteria: lady-git via shell-out git: list_worktrees(repo) -> Vec<Worktree { path, branch, head, locked }>, add_worktree(path, branch_or_new), remove_worktree(path), prune_worktrees()
ui: a worktrees panel listing them with add (path + branch/new-branch) and remove; opening a worktree opens it as a repo tab (reuses Phase 1 repo manager)
Unit test: add a worktree in a tempdir repo, assert it lists, then remove it; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green

userStories.title: Reflog view + restore lost commits
userStories.description: As a user, I want to browse the reflog and recover lost commits.
acceptanceCriteria: lady-git: reflog(repo, ref) -> Vec<ReflogEntry { oid, prev_oid, action, message, time }> (via gix or `git reflog --format`)
ui: a reflog view listing entries; actions to create a branch at, or checkout, a chosen entry's oid (restore)

Unit test: make + reset a commit in a tempdir repo, assert the dangling oid appears in the reflog and a branch can be created at it; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green

userStories.title: Bisect
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run git bisect to find a bad commit.
acceptanceCriteria: lady-git via shell-out git: bisect_start(repo, bad, good), bisect_mark(good|bad|skip), bisect_reset(repo), bisect_state(repo) -> { current_oid, remaining_steps_estimate, suspected: Option<Oid> }
ui: a bisect panel showing current commit under test, good/bad/skip buttons, remaining-steps estimate, and the suspected commit when found; reset exits bisect
Unit test: scripted bisect over a tempdir repo with a known bad commit converges to it; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green

userStories.title: Custom commands (UI builder)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to define and run my own git commands with typed inputs.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings-persisted custom commands: { name, template } where template has typed placeholders — free text, branch combo, file selector (PLAN.md §1)
ui: a builder to create/edit a custom command and a runner that prompts for the placeholders, executes against the current repo (shell-out), and shows stdout/stderr + exit code
Placeholders are substituted safely (argument vector, not string-concatenated into a shell) to avoid injection
Unit test on placeholder parsing + safe argv substitution; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §1 custom commands. Security: build an argv, never interpolate user input into a shell string.

userStories.title: External diff/merge tool integration
userStories.description: As a user, I want to open a file or conflict in my configured external tool.
acceptanceCriteria: lady-git: launch_difftool(repo, spec) and launch_mergetool(repo, path) honoring the user's git config (diff.tool/merge.tool, difftool.<t>.cmd) or a Lady setting
ui: 'Open in external diff tool' on a file diff and 'Open in external merge tool' on a conflicted file (PH3-001)
Graceful error when no tool is configured; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/build green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green; manual window note in progress.txt
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §1 external diff/merge tools. Reuse git's tool config where possible (ADR-0003 spirit).

userStories.title: lady-hosting crate + GitHub auth
userStories.description: As a user, I want Lady to authenticate with GitHub so it can act on my behalf.
acceptanceCriteria: New crate crates/lady-hosting: a HostingProvider trait + a GitHub impl (reqwest, rustls); detect a GitHub remote from the repo's remotes
Auth: OAuth device flow OR a PAT; the token is stored in the OS keychain via keyring (ADR-0006 — hosting-API tokens only), never on disk/plaintext/logs
Tauri commands: github_auth_start / github_auth_status / github_sign_out; ui surfaces connect/disconnect in settings

Unit test the remote-detection + token storage abstraction with a mocked keychain/HTTP (wiremock); cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green; cargo deny passes (reqwest/rustls are permissive); npm run build green
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §7. GitHub only at v1; other forges are Fast-follow (Phase 4). Never log tokens.

userStories.title: Create GitHub pull request from a branch
userStories.description: As a user, I want to open a PR for my branch without leaving Lady.
acceptanceCriteria: lady-hosting: create_pull_request(repo, { head, base, title, body, draft }) -> PR url via the GitHub API using the PH3-011 token
ui: a branch context-menu 'Create Pull Request' opening a form (title/body/base, prefilled from branch + recent commit subjects); on success show the PR url and offer to open it in the browser
Clear errors for no-auth / no-GitHub-remote / API failure (e.g. PR already exists)
Unit test create_pull_request against a mocked GitHub API (wiremock); cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green; manual window note in progress.txt
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §1/§7 — matches Fork's branch-context-menu PR creation. Release-blocker for Core Parity.

userStories.title: Licensing gate — trial + offline signed-key verify
userStories.description: As the business, I need the paid client gated by a trial and an offline license key (ADR-0007).
acceptanceCriteria: New module (e.g. crates/lady-license): verify an Ed25519-signed license payload against an embedded public key; reject tampered/expired/wrong-product keys; record first-run timestamp in the platform config dir for a 30-day trial
App states: Trial (N days left) -> Expired (gated) -> Licensed (valid key entered); a license-entry + trial-status UI; gating blocks the main UI when expired/unlicensed
Security: client-side only, explicitly NOT DRM (ADR-0007) — never gate security-sensitive paths or embed protected secrets behind it; document this in code comments

Unit tests: generate a throwaway Ed25519 keypair, sign a valid license -> verifies; tamper a byte -> rejected; expired key -> rejected; trial countdown math; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green
userStories.notes: ADR-0007 — release-blocking, in Core Parity scope. Use an ed25519 crate (e.g. ed25519-dalek, permissive). The real signing private key stays offline; tests use an ephemeral one.

userStories.title: Core Parity release-readiness gate (Phase 3 EXIT)
userStories.description: As the team, I want to confirm Lady is feature-complete for v1.0 before the release line.
acceptanceCriteria: A docs/RELEASE-CHECKLIST.md enumerating the Core Parity surface (CONTEXT.md) with each item ticked to its implementing story across Phases 1-3, and explicitly listing the Fast-follow exclusions (LFS, git-flow, submodule edges, non-GitHub forge PRs)
Whole workspace green: cargo fmt --all --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, cargo deny check, npm run build — all pass
Version bumped to 1.0.0-rc across the workspace + tauri.conf.json
progress.txt notes that PACKAGING / notarization / auto-update are Phase 6 (this gate is feature-completeness, not the actual ship)
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §0 Core Parity + §9 Phase 3 exit. Confirms the RELEASE LINE; actual packaging is Phase 6. After this, the next PRD is Phase 4 (Fast-follow) or Phase 5 (AI).
